surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `high priority`. Maintainer `high priority` label confirms the bug is important and acknowledged.

linked_prs.title: [cuDNN][Convolution] Disable cuDNN for 3D convolutions with kernel size != 1 for cuDNN 9.8+

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: Thanks, I think the failure is visible in 9.8 as well. Are you able to upgrade to a current nightly as I see the failure seems to go away in 9.13? Will forward to the cuDNN team and work bypassing cuDNN For 9.8-9.10 in the meantime.

confidence_justification: Step 1: 'already_fixed' blocking signal present — PR #164027 merged 2025-09-29 as a workaround disabling the cuDNN path for 3D convolutions on cuDNN 9.8+. Per the precedence rule: skip all other signals and set confidence to 0.05. The root cause is a cuDNN library bug in versions 9.8-9.10 which 'seems to go away in 9.13' per COLLABORATOR eqy. The PyTorch-side workaround has landed. Final: 0.05.
ground_truth_delta: Unlike positive_71774 (which has an approved-but-unmerged fix that needs revival), pytorch_163539 already has a merged workaround PR #164027. The root cause lies in the cuDNN 9.8-9.10 library itself — not in PyTorch code — and eqy confirmed the issue disappears in cuDNN 9.13. A contributor cannot fix a bug in an external library. The issue remains OPEN only as a tracking entry while the cuDNN team addresses the upstream defect.
